narratives.evidence_quote: tyrannical “climate” policies of the World Economic Forum (WEF).
narratives.reasoning: The text frames the WEF’s climate policies as tyrannical and uses language that portrays policy as a fearsome plan (rationing etc.), which matches the narrative of amplifying climate fears.

narratives.evidence_quote: climate change ‘is clearly an obsession of the very rich and highly educated.’
narratives.reasoning: The text quotes a source saying climate change is an obsession of elites, presenting a critical view of the climate movement and its leaders.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: CC: Amplifying Climate Fears: Amplifying existing fears of global warming
subnarratives.evidence_quote: almost 90 percent of “American elites” believe strict rationing of gas, meat, and electricity is necessary to combat the so-called “climate crisis.”
subnarratives.reasoning: The quote frames climate change as an urgent crisis and advocates drastic policy measures (rationing) to 'combat' it, indicating fear-based amplification of concerns about global warming.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: CC: Criticism of climate movement: Other
subnarratives.evidence_quote: A disturbing new report has emerged revealing that an overwhelming number of so-called “American elites” support the tyrannical “climate” policies of the World Economic Forum (WEF).
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote directly frames climate policies as tyrannical and characterizes the climate movement as problematic by presenting elite support as evidence of a dangerous or oppressive policy agenda.
